assertionResults.reason: A dedicated proof surface ('Built and Shipping') appears immediately after the hero and before the roadmap section, showing live product evidence before dense roadmap detail.

observations: DOM section order placed #proof at ~1657px and #roadmap at ~3745px, with #live-now between them.
The proof surface lists live terminal, on-chain access gating, real copy trading, and Lite Mode with BETTER Docs proof links.
evidence.console: No page errors. Console showed only dev-mode info messages for React DevTools and HMR connection.
evidence.network: No API/XHR activity was required for the in-page anchor flow.

assertionResults.reason: The first-visit flow explains BETTER in plain language, names traders/newcomers/whales as the audience, distinguishes Live vs Planned in the hero, and reaches proof/live sections before roadmap detail.

observations: Hero copy: 'BETTER is a prediction-market intelligence platform...' with audience framing 'from curious newcomers to committed whales'.
Hero status chips and caveat separate live reality from future ambition before scroll, then the first scroll reaches the proof surface and live-now section ahead of roadmap content.
evidence.console: No page errors. Console showed only dev-mode info messages for React DevTools and HMR connection.
evidence.network: No API/XHR activity was required for the in-page anchor flow.

assertionResults.reason: The early landing hierarchy is single-purpose and proof-led: hero explains BETTER, proof section demonstrates shipped reality, live-now inventories current scope, then maturity labels prepare users for roadmap exploration.

observations: Early section order observed: #what-is-better -> #proof -> #live-now -> maturity-label explainer -> #roadmap.
The roadmap does not appear until after the proof and live-now surfaces, so deeper exploration follows rather than competes with the early proof sequence.
evidence.console: No page errors. Console showed only dev-mode info messages for React DevTools and HMR connection.
evidence.network: No API/XHR activity was required for the in-page anchor flow.

assertionResults.reason: The most visually prominent hero CTA is the filled 'See What's Live' button, and clicking it moves to the live-now section before any roadmap exploration path.

observations: Hero CTA inventory showed primary 'See What's Live' linking to #live-now and secondary 'Explore the Roadmap' linking to #roadmap.
Clicking the primary CTA updated the URL to http://127.0.0.1:3100/#live-now and landed on the 'What's Live Today' section.
evidence.console: No page errors. Console showed only dev-mode info messages for React DevTools and HMR connection.
evidence.network: No API/XHR activity was required for the in-page anchor flow.

assertionResults.reason: CTA labels and destinations stay aligned across hero, proof, nav, and roadmap entry paths: live-oriented CTAs point to #live-now or BETTER Docs, while exploratory CTAs point to #roadmap.

observations: Extracted CTA map: hero 'See What's Live' -> #live-now; hero 'Explore the Roadmap' -> #roadmap; proof 'See Full Live Scope' -> #live-now; proof 'Read the Docs' -> https://docs.betteragent.ai; nav 'Live Now' -> #live-now.
Clicking the proof CTA produced #live-now and clicking the secondary hero CTA produced #roadmap, matching their promises without switching maturity framing.
evidence.console: No page errors. Console showed only dev-mode info messages for React DevTools and HMR connection.
evidence.network: No API/XHR activity was required for the in-page anchor flow.

summary: Validated 5 proof/CTA assertions on the live landing page. All 5 passed: the page is proof-led before roadmap detail, first-visit framing is fast and clear, the early hierarchy stays single-purpose, the primary hero CTA goes to live scope first, and CTA promises remain consistent across hero/proof/navigation surfaces. No runtime errors were observed; console output was limited to development-only React DevTools/HMR notices.
